behaviors.description: Exponential backoff delay doubles on each consecutive failure and is capped at configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local retry-delay math/state, with no module handoff required (MOCK-MISS: no). REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP are all no. Higher-level rejection: behavior text is only about delay progression/cap, not transport/timers/network.
behaviors.test_description: Test the backoff calculator/state object directly: feed consecutive failure counts (1..N), assert delays follow 2^k growth and never exceed maxDelay.

behaviors.description: Retry logic recognizes rate-limit responses and computes correct wait before next attempt.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at module boundary between response classification and retry scheduler (MOCK-MISS: yes for cross-module handoff). REAL-INFRA no: in-process stubbed HTTP responses reproduce status/headers exactly. Higher-level rejection: behavior text does not require real network timeouts/socket behavior, only identifying rate-limit response + wait computation.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry policy + response parser + scheduler with a stub transport returning 429/headers; assert retry decision is 'retry' and computed delay matches policy (including Retry-After precedence if supported).

behaviors.description: After waiting, client resends the original request and transparently returns the eventual success result.
behaviors.justification: Needs orchestration across timer/backoff + transport + result propagation (MOCK-MISS: yes at module interaction). REAL-INFRA no: fake timers plus stub transport can expose resend/return-path bugs. Higher-level rejection: behavior text does not require real OS/network primitives beyond async scheduling.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake clock and stub transport: first response rate-limited, second response success. Assert same request payload is resent after scheduled delay and `call()` resolves to the success payload with no retry details leaked.

behaviors.description: When max retries is reached, client returns `QuotaExhaustedError` (typed) instead of generic error.
behaviors.justification: Typed error is emitted by retry-loop termination path, requiring interaction of retry counter + error mapper (MOCK-MISS: yes). REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP are no. Higher-level rejection: behavior text is about local error typing at retry cap, not real subprocess/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Configure low max retries, stub transport to always return rate-limit failure, run `call()`, and assert rejection is instance/type `QuotaExhaustedError` with expected fields (and not generic Error).

behaviors.description: Entire retry chain (initial try, backoff retries, quota tracking) terminates and never loops indefinitely before surfacing error.
behaviors.justification: This is a multi-module control-flow invariant (retry orchestrator + quota tracker + termination guard), so unit is too low (MOCK-MISS: yes). Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required; deterministic stubs + fake timers can reproduce infinite-loop risk. Higher-level rejection: behavior text does not require real external infrastructure, only chain termination correctness.
behaviors.test_description: With fake timers and always-rate-limited stub transport, execute `call()` and advance time; assert attempt count stops at configured bound, promise settles with expected terminal error, and no additional retries are scheduled after settlement.

behaviors.description: Concurrent async callers in one process consume from a shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears only when multiple callers interact through shared in-process state (MOCK-MISS: yes). REAL-INFRA no: true concurrency at JS async/task level is reproducible in-process without real network. Higher-level rejection: behavior text scopes to same-process shared bucket, not distributed/system coordination.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate one client/shared quota manager; fire multiple concurrent `call()` invocations with controlled stub transport; assert aggregate quota consumption reflects one shared bucket (not per-caller buckets) and later callers are throttled accordingly.

behaviors.description: Queued waiting requests are dispatched FIFO in original submission order.
behaviors.justification: Ordering bug arises in queue + dispatcher interaction under async contention (MOCK-MISS: yes). REAL-INFRA no: in-process scheduler with fake timers captures ordering failures. Higher-level rejection: behavior text is queue discipline within library, not OS/network-level ordering guarantees.
behaviors.test_description: Submit multiple requests rapidly while quota blocks immediate send; release capacity stepwise via stub responses/time advances; assert outbound dispatch order matches submission order exactly.
